content: Existing Self-Refine frameworks, which rely on single-perspective iterative self-feedback, can be upgraded by integrating multiple inconsistent self-reflection perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a multi-view self-refine framework that avoids local optimization traps from single-perspective feedback. This hybrid framework retains Self-Refine’s core advantage of requiring no extra training or labeled data while leveraging the contrast of inconsistent perspectives to surface hidden flaws an LLM would miss when evaluating its output from only one frame of reference.
